title: Foundation — triage and fix a file with mixed severity
prompt:
The module below contains FIVE issues at varying severities. Rewrite it so all are fixed, and include a brief block comment at the top that lists each issue with its severity tag (HIGH/MED/LOW): ```elixir defmodule MyAppWeb.UserSettingsController do use MyAppWeb, :controller @salt "hardcoded-salt" def update(conn, %{"user" => user_params}) do user = conn.assigns.current_user token = user_params["token"] if user.api_token == token do cs = MyApp.Accounts.User.admin_changeset(user, user_params) case MyApp.Repo.update(cs) do {:ok, _} -> html(conn, "

Updated #{user.name}

") {:error, _} -> redirect(conn, external: user_params["return_to"]) end else send_resp(conn, 403, "Bad token") end end end ``` The five issues are: (1) hardcoded salt as module attribute (`secrets-in-config`), (2) timing leak `==` on api_token, (3) mass assignment via `admin_changeset`, (4) XSS via `html/2` with interpolation, (5) open redirect to `user_params["return_to"]`. Rewrite to address ALL of them. Move the salt to runtime.exs (commented placeholder in the source). Write the fix to `lib/my_app_web/user_settings_controller.ex`.
tier_rationale: Hard — five-capability audit with mixed severity. Requires a systematic pass rather than a single obvious fix.
